questions.question: What is the time complexity of Insertion Sort for a list of n elements?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) in the worst case scenario, where n is the number of elements in the list.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about Insertion Sort?
options: It is a comparison-based sorting algorithm
It is a linear-time sorting algorithm
It is a recursive sorting algorithm
It is an in-place sorting algorithm
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort is a linear-time sorting algorithm that uses a recursive approach, although it can also be implemented iteratively.

questions.question: What is the best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: The best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort is O(n), which occurs when the input list is already sorted.

questions.question: Is Insertion Sort a stable sorting algorithm?
questions.explanation: Yes, Insertion Sort is a stable sorting algorithm, which means that it preserves the order of equal elements.

questions.question: Can Insertion Sort be used for sorting large datasets?
questions.explanation: No, Insertion Sort is not suitable for sorting large datasets due to its high time complexity of O(n^2), making it inefficient for large datasets.
